name: Bob Dorough
abstract: Robert Lrod Dorough (December 12, 1923 – April 23, 2018) was an American bebop and cool jazz vocalist, pianist, and composer. He became famous as the composer and performer of songs in the TV series Schoolhouse Rock!, as well as for his work with Miles Davis,  Blossom Dearie, and others.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: That was Bob Dorough’s baby... Dorough is a particular hero of mine. [INTERVIEWER] Yeah. you guys have collaborated a number of times.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: So I had one big band, then a quartet album with Bob Dorough and some other people
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: I sing “Conjunction Junction” and “I’m Just a Bill” and whole bunch of those things, because of Bob Dorough and Dave Frishberg. They started writing them twenty years ago or so.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: There were a number of young singer-songwriters in New York at the time, all very witty, very capable, very verbal, like Dave Frishberg, Bob Dorough...
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: I can remember going to listen to I think it was Joe Lovano and Bob Dorough together at Birdland, maybe fifteen years ago, maybe more.
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, November 11, 2017 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I worked -- I'm going to tell you one gig with Bob Dorough... Bob Dorough called me up... They had a bar mitzvah to play out in
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
